Creates a yeet configuration file with sensible defaults.

	By default this writes .yeet.yaml at the repository root when inside a git
	repository, or in the current directory otherwise. Use --config to write a
	different path.

Usage:
  yeet init [flags]

Examples:
  yeet init
  yeet init --config .yeet.release.yaml

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for init

Global Flags:
      --config string       path to config file (default: nearest ancestor .yeet.yaml)
      --log-format string   set log output format: text|json (default "text")
      --quiet               show warnings and errors only
  -v, --verbose             enable debug logging
